definitions.text: An uncertain or unspecified thing; one thing.
examples: I must have forgotten to pack something, but I can't think what.
I have something for you in my bag.

definitions.text: A quality to a moderate degree.
examples: The performance was something of a disappointment.
That child is something of a genius.

definitions.text: A talent or quality that is difficult to specify.
Having a characteristic that the speaker cannot specify.

examples: The baby looks something like his father.

definitions.text: Used to adverbialise a following adjective
examples: I miss them something terrible/rotten. (I miss them terribly)

definitions.text: Designates an action whose name is forgotten by, unknown or unimportant to the user, e.g. from words of a song.
An object whose nature is yet to be defined.
An object whose name is forgotten by, unknown or unimportant to the user, e.g., from words of a song. Also used to refer to an object earlier indefinitely referred to as 'something' (pronoun sense).
